description: Temporary, governed exemptions from the api/api-client contract-mirror requirement. Every entry must name its planned removal milestone; the gate fails on missing fields and on stale entries (module mirrored or gone). This is visible debt, not hidden debt: the gate prints this table on every run.

exemptions.reason: Authentication contracts gain their typed client mirror when the web shells implement client-facing login/registration flows. M2.3 built server-side authorization infrastructure only (no client auth surface).
Decision logic for the client-facing auth flows landing with the web shells (M7.1).

Tenancy access contracts gain their client mirror when the web shells implement workspace display/switch. M2.2/M2.3 consume tenancy server-side (ActingContext resolver + session-context endpoint); no client tenancy surface yet.
Tenancy evaluation handlers for the client-facing workspace flows landing with the web shells (M7.1).
Audit workflows are unified onto the outbox dispatcher before any client surface exists.
Break-glass workflows gain their surface with the persisted break-glass flow.
